1.name: Cutpurse
1.flavor: First steel of a guttersnipe's trade, light to palm and cheap to abandon in flight.

2.name: Quicksilver
2.flavor: Quenched to a liquid sheen, it slips aside as readily as it slips between ribs.

3.name: Gloomfang
3.flavor: Its edge swims with gloom, and every wound it opens steals the daylight from a foe's eyes.

4.name: Hushblade
4.flavor: Smith-wrought to throttle the throat; a chant dies unspoken the instant it draws blood.

5.name: Argent Dirk
5.flavor: Balanced to the grain and burnished bright, the dirk a silversmith sets aside for himself.

6.name: Sanguine Gauche
6.flavor: A duelist's parrying knife that drinks the blood it turns aside to mend its own bearer.

7.name: Adamant Fang
7.flavor: Honed past sense into a tooth of cold adamant: it bites to the bone and shields nothing.

8.name: Mortal Coil
8.flavor: One graze, and the appointed hour begins its quiet count; what it marks, death collects.

9.name: Galewind
9.flavor: A gust lies coiled in its hollow blade, loosed to harry the foe with every keening stroke.

10.name: Zwill Straightblade
10.flavor: A knife manufactured in a region renowned for its blades.

11.name: Swiftfang
11.flavor: So weightless it leads the hand rather than follows; swifter the wielder, crueler the cut.

12.name: Mistedge
12.flavor: A throwing blade balanced for the long cast, finding its mark a pace beyond a foe's reach.

13.name: Frost Kodachi
13.flavor: Tempered in winter's heart, its kiss leaves frost, and a deeper cold where it sinks.

14.name: Raijin Longblade
14.flavor: Christened for the thunder god, it carries his storm and looses it at the touch of flesh.

15.name: Silentfang
15.flavor: Drawn against the spellbound, it stops a chant mid-breath; the silence finishes the deed.

17.name: Iga Blade
17.flavor: An exquisitely crafted ninja sword that was once used by a secret society.

18.name: Koga Blade
18.flavor: A powerful ninja sword that was once used by a secret society.

19.name: Vagabond
19.flavor: A wanderer's blade worn smooth by the road, slim enough to slip a blow before it falls.

20.name: Cleaver
20.flavor: Broad and heavy at the edge, it parries nothing; it drives a foe back off their feet.

21.name: Riposte
21.flavor: A duelist's sword balanced to catch an incoming stroke and return it in the same breath.

22.name: Claymore
22.flavor: A two-handed greatsword whose reach lets its bearer cut a pace beyond the foe's swing.

23.name: Sanguine Sword
23.flavor: The blade of a sellsword who supped on the lives he felled, till the field claimed him.

24.name: Stormbrand
24.flavor: Drawn from coral and storm alike, its edge mutters with thunder waiting to leap free.

25.name: Tanglethorn
25.flavor: A briar-marked blade said to send roots through any it strikes, fixing them to the earth.

26.name: Flamberge
26.flavor: A waving, flame-shaped edge that kindles where it lands, leaving the unclean to smolder.

27.name: Wrathblade
27.flavor: Forged for the cornered and bleeding; nearer to ruin, the hungrier its edge grows.

28.name: Swiftedge
28.flavor: A keen blade that answers the swift hand alone, biting harder the quicker its bearer goes.

29.name: Graviton
29.flavor: A dull, leaden edge that calls down a crushing weight, dragging the proudest foe down.

30.name: Arcanum
30.flavor: Runes crawl its fuller, and at a touch unwind whatever wards a foe wraps about itself.

31.name: Lightbringer
31.flavor: Hallowed steel that wakes with a dawn-white glow, scouring shadow and dead flesh away.

32.name: Materia Blade
32.flavor: The sword belonging to Cloud, the extraplanar wanderer. It was designed for members of SOLDIER, allowing them to fight to their full potential.

33.name: Defender
33.flavor: A broad-bladed knight's sword with jewels embedded in its pommel.

34.name: Save the Queen
34.flavor: A knight's sword given as a symbol of an oath of fealty. It has been blessed with a ward of protection.

35.name: Excalibur
35.flavor: It is said that only the true heir to the throne can wield this legendary knight's sword. The worthy will be granted fleetness of foot and divine might.

36.name: Ragnarok
36.flavor: This knight's sword is mentioned in tales about the end of the world. It provides defense against spellcraft.

37.name: Chaos Blade
37.flavor: This holy knight's sword is said to have been a gift from the gods themselves, and possesses mystical powers.

38.name: Ashura
38.flavor: A katana with a brilliant white blade.

39.name: Kotetsu
39.flavor: A katana whose blade is adorned with a breathtaking carving of a tiger.

40.name: Bizen Osafune
40.flavor: A katana with a blade forged of the finest iron sand by master swordsmith Nagamitsu Osafune, of the eastern land of Bizen.

41.name: Murasame
41.flavor: Referred to as a demon sword, this katana's keen blade showers blood upon the battlefield.

42.name: Ame-no-Murakumo
42.flavor: This katana's design was inspired by a legendary blade said to have been plucked from the tail of a dragon that marauded eastern lands.

43.name: Kiyomori
43.flavor: This exquisitely crafted katana has a beautifully patterned blade and an incredible cutting edge.

44.name: Muramasa
44.flavor: This katana is said to have drunk the blood of countless enemies.

45.name: Kiku-ichimonji
45.flavor: A katana with a hilt in the shape of a chrysanthemum.

46.name: Masamune
46.flavor: This katana was forged by a master swordsmith, and its sheath is inlaid with beautiful patterns.

47.name: Chirijiraden
47.flavor: This katana is decorated with intricate designs and boasts a wickedly sharp edge.

48.name: Terrastaff
48.flavor: Quarried from old bedrock, it strikes with the slow patience of shifting earth.

49.name: Ravager
49.flavor: A savage cleaving thing that pours its own blind frenzy into every wound it tears open.

50.name: Sunderer
50.flavor: A spiteful edge made less to slay than to ruin, shattering the arms off whatever it meets.

51.name: Wellspring Rod
51.flavor: An apprentice's rod that drinks a foe's magic dry at every tap, never wanting for power.

52.name: Spark Rod
52.flavor: A rod that holds a fistful of caged storm; each blow lets the thunder out for the asking.

53.name: Ember Rod
53.flavor: Embers smolder in its heart, eager to leap; a touch is enough to set a foe ablaze.

54.name: Frost Rod
54.flavor: Its tip is sheathed in everlasting rime; where it points, the air turns to killing winter.

55.name: Hushward Rod
55.flavor: A warded rod that keeps its bearer's voice when every other throat in the field is hushed.

56.name: Umbral Rod
56.flavor: Carved from nightwood and steeped in shadow; the dark answers eagerly to who wields it.

57.name: Dragon Rod
57.flavor: A rod wielded by a dragon master. Its tip is made from carved dragon bone.

58.name: Rod of Faith
58.flavor: This rod purges doubt from those it touches, turning them into pillars of faith.

59.name: Birchwood Staff
59.flavor: A plain length of green birch, the first staff pressed into any apprentice's hands.

60.name: Warlock's Staff
60.flavor: Bound with a warlock's hex, it turns hostile spells back upon the throats that spoke them.

61.name: Mending Staff
61.flavor: White-lacquered and consecrated, its touch knits living flesh and withers the dead.

62.name: Warding Staff
62.flavor: A consecrated stave that lets no enchantment seal its bearer's lips, however loud the din.

63.name: Blazing Staff
63.flavor: Its crown smolders with caged flame; the warmth keeps restless feet quick afield.

64.name: Sanctus Staff
64.flavor: A gilded stave that gathers sacred light and gives it back tenfold to those who pray.

65.name: Zeus Mace
65.flavor: A staff that increases the might of its user.

66.name: Staff of the Magi
66.flavor: An ordinary-looking wooden stick with a curious stone embedded in its tip.

67.name: Warbrand
67.flavor: A graceless slab of war-iron with no guile to it, only a weight that breaks what it meets.

68.name: Bloodlash
68.flavor: Cruelly serrated and slick with venom; it leaves no clean cut, only a wound that festers.

69.name: Climhazzard
69.flavor: A merciful stroke for the dying; it glances off the hale yet guts the already-bleeding.

70.name: Sasori
70.flavor: A barbed, scorpion-named katana whose every sting marks a foe for a slow, certain death.

71.name: Outrider Pistol
71.flavor: A plain wheel-lock that throws its shot farther than any, asking nothing of the hand.

72.name: Ironclad Repeater
72.flavor: An iron-framed repeater for the long road; it hits harder than the pistol and never jams.

73.name: Stoneshooter
73.flavor: A heavy gun bored to spit earthen shards; each shot lands like a sliding mountain.

74.name: Glacial Gun
74.flavor: A magickal firearm that shoots freezing bullets.

75.name: Blaze Gun
75.flavor: A magickal firearm that shoots flaming bullets.

76.name: Blaster
76.flavor: A magickal firearm that shoots electrically charged bullets.

77.name: Scoutbolt
77.flavor: A featherlight crossbow a scout slings on one shoulder, drawn quick and loosed quicker.

78.name: Eclipsebolt
78.flavor: A bolt meant for slaying elites; its quarrel marks a foe, who sees no further dusk.

79.name: Arbalest
79.flavor: A steel arbalest cranked taut as a war-engine, its shaft passing clean through any foe.

80.name: Venombolt
80.flavor: Its quarrels glisten with a slow venom that creeps the wound long after the bolt is drawn.

81.name: Pitchbolt
81.flavor: A crossbow whose bolts burst with clinging pitch, leaving the quarry slick for the spark.

82.name: Siegebolt
82.flavor: A siege-grade crossbow scaled for a strong back, driving a quarrel to crack a shield wall.

83.name: Skirmisher
83.flavor: A slender hunting bow that prizes the quick step; loosed and gone before the answer comes.

84.name: Windrunner
84.flavor: Worked from pale silver, light as nothing, it dances clear of every blow without a trick.

85.name: Frostarc
85.flavor: Carved from rimed wood that never thaws; its arrows carry the chill of a frozen lake.

86.name: Stormarc
86.flavor: Strung with a wire that hums before it sings, each shaft crackling stormlight to its mark.

87.name: Skypiercer
87.flavor: A tall bow that lofts arrows on the wind, arcing over crag to pluck a foe from any perch.

88.name: Tidecaller
88.flavor: A bow whose shaft hisses like surf and stills the throat it finds, drowning words mid-cry.

89.name: Huntress
89.flavor: The mark of a true marksman, balanced so fine that hand and target seem joined in flight.

90.name: Yoichi Bow
90.flavor: The bow of a legendary archer. Its great size allows it to generate incredible power.

91.name: Perseus Bow
91.flavor: The bow of a mythical hero. It is constructed entirely of metal and requires great strength to draw.

92.name: Siren's Lyre
92.flavor: A harp whose airs ride the gathering wind, luring the unwary into a fog of confusion.

93.name: Duskstring Harp
93.flavor: Strung for twilight songs that smear a listener's sight to dusk as they stand entranced.

94.name: Faerie Harp
94.flavor: The pure sound of this harp can charm those who hear it played.

95.name: Glarebound Tome
95.flavor: A street orator's folio, its arguments so dazzling they leave the heckler blinking, lost.

96.name: Knell Codex
96.flavor: A codex of beasts and their endings; to be named in its pages is to be promised the grave.

97.name: Binding Codex
97.flavor: A codex of binding sigils that fix a reader's feet fast, rooted by the words' own weight.

98.name: Omnilex
98.flavor: A book said to contain every word of every known language.

99.name: Skewer
99.flavor: A plain ash spear cut to reach a foe a pace beyond a sword's swing, and nothing more.

100.name: Footman's Spear
100.flavor: Issued to every footsoldier; it thrusts clean from the second rank, out of the press.

101.name: Frostpoint
101.flavor: Ice rimes the spearhead and clings, so a deep thrust leaves frost glittering in the wound.

102.name: Stormpike
102.flavor: A charged partisan that hums before it strikes, calling a bolt down as steel meets flesh.

103.name: Tombspire
103.flavor: Grave-marker carved and far of reach; it spares the hale, but pins the bleeding to finish.

104.name: Holy Lance
104.flavor: This sacred weapon shines with a pure and brilliant light.

105.name: Dragon Whisker
105.flavor: A spear made from a mysterious material that some say is the whisker of a dragon.

106.name: Wyrmpike
106.flavor: A two-handed pike light as a squall; it tags a far mark, then doom gathers like wind.

107.name: Greenwood Pole
107.flavor: Cut from green wood for the unblooded; a focused blow flings martial spirit a pace off.

108.name: Ironreed Pole
108.flavor: Reed banded with iron, springy and swift, suited to a hand that prizes speed over weight.

109.name: Slumber Rod
109.flavor: Wax-scented, slow to swing; its measured taps coax the watchful into heavy-lidded sleep.

110.name: Hushfan
110.flavor: A folded-iron war-fan that beats the air shut round a chanting mouth, stealing its spell.

111.name: Sage's Pole
111.flavor: Worn smooth by a sage's grip; no trick to it, only the weight of a practiced strike.

112.name: Ivory Pole
112.flavor: A pole made from ivory that has been specially treated for improved durability.

113.name: Eight-Fluted Pole
113.flavor: Eight grooves channel a slow venom, so one clean rap festers long after the bout is won.

114.name: Whale Whisker
114.flavor: A pole made from an unidentified material that some say is a whale's whisker.

115.name: Sandman Satchel
115.flavor: A conjurer's satchel that breathes slumberdust, sinking the wakeful into untroubled dream.

116.name: Fallingstar Bag
116.flavor: A custom-made handbag designed for battle.

117.name: Hornet Pouch
117.flavor: A pouch that looses a sting at the lash, festering in the wound long after the blow.

118.name: Hexweave Bag
118.flavor: Woven with binding hexes; one swing snares a voice and strangles the spell mid-word.

119.name: Tarsilk Veil
119.flavor: Tarred silk that clings where it lands, leaving the mark slick and waiting for a spark.

120.name: Bindsilk Veil
120.flavor: Gossamer veil snapping taut round a quarry's limbs, rooting it as its dancer slips aside.

121.name: Gravesilk Veil
121.flavor: Funeral silk falling with the weight of a tomb; beneath its hem the mighty buckle and sag.
